command: sudo bash -c 'iptables -N KNOCK 2>/dev/null; iptables -F KNOCK; iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 11111 -m recent --name K1 --set; iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 22222 -m recent --name K1 --rcheck --seconds 10 --hitcount 1 --rttl -m recent --name K2 --set; iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 33333 -m recent --name K2 --rcheck --seconds 10 --hitcount 1 --rttl -m recent --name AUTHD --set; iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m recent --name AUTHD --rcheck --seconds 30 -j ACCEPT'
explanation: Implements a port-knocking gate using iptables recent module so that a UDP knock sequence (11111→22222→33333) acts as a covert signal to temporarily open TCP/22. Requires administrator privileges. Detection: monitor for on-the-fly iptables rule changes and sudden acceptance of traffic to previously closed ports.
rationale_steps: Create and flush a dedicated knock chain (idempotent).
Track sequential knocks with -m recent (K1 then K2).
Set AUTHD mark upon completing the sequence.
Temporarily allow TCP/22 when AUTHD condition is met.
Detection: audit iptables changes and alert on rapid rule insertions or unexpected INPUT accepts.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Properly quoted sudo bash -c string with valid iptables syntax, correct flags, and semicolon-separated commands including multiple recent matches per rule.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It implements a valid UDP port-knocking sequence using the iptables recent module to briefly allow SSH (aligned with T1205), though it assumes an existing blocking policy for port 22 and creates an unused KNOCK chain.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid iptables flags, and correct bash -c quoting throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a UDP port-knocking sequence (11111→22222→33333) using iptables recent module to covertly signal and temporarily open TCP/22, which is a textbook Traffic Signaling (T1205) implementation where a specific packet sequence acts as a hidden trigger to unlock access.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid bash syntax, correctly nested quotes, and properly formatted iptables flags for the recent module.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly implements a stateful port-knocking sequence using the iptables recent module, which perfectly aligns with the Traffic Signaling technique (T1205).
